title: VIZ Blockchain JSON-RPC API Specification
description: Complete specification of all JSON-RPC methods exposed by VIZ node plugins. Intended as a machine-readable spec for API explorer generation.

plugins.name: validator_api
plugins.description: Provides read-only access to validator (witness) data: schedules, votes, and validator registration info.

methods.description: Returns the list of currently active validator account names that are participating in block production.

returns.description: Array of account names of currently active validators.

methods.description: Returns the current validator schedule object, including the shuffled list of validators and their timeshares.

returns.description: The validator_schedule_object containing current_shuffled_validators, timeshare, and related scheduling data.

methods.description: Returns a list of validator objects by their database IDs. For each ID, returns either the validator_api_object or null if not found.

params.name: validator_ids
params.caption: Validator IDs
params.description: Array of validator object database IDs to look up.

returns.description: Array of optional validator_api_object entries, one per requested ID.

methods.description: Returns the validator object registered under a specific account name, or null if the account is not a validator.

params.name: account_name
params.caption: Account Name
params.description: The account name to look up as a validator.

returns.description: The validator_api_object for the account, or null if not a validator.

methods.description: Returns validators sorted by total votes (descending). Starts from a given account name. Only returns validators with votes > 0. Maximum 100 results.

params.name: from
params.caption: From Account
params.description: The account name to start from. Use empty string to start from the top.

params.name: limit
params.caption: Limit
params.description: Maximum number of results to return. Must not exceed 100.

returns.description: Array of validator_api_object entries sorted by vote count.

methods.description: Returns validators sorted by counted votes (descending). Starts from a given account name. Only returns validators with counted_votes > 0. Maximum 100 results.

params.name: from
params.caption: From Account
params.description: The account name to start from. Use empty string to start from the top.

params.name: limit
params.caption: Limit
params.description: Maximum number of results to return. Must not exceed 100.

returns.description: Array of validator_api_object entries sorted by counted vote.

methods.description: Returns the total number of registered validators on the blockchain.

returns.description: Total count of registered validators.

methods.description: Looks up validator account names starting from a lower bound. Returns up to 1000 results alphabetically.

params.name: lower_bound_name
params.caption: Lower Bound Name
params.description: The lower bound of the first account name to return. Use empty string to start from the beginning.

params.name: limit
params.caption: Limit
params.description: Maximum number of results to return. Must not exceed 1000.

returns.description: Set of validator account names matching the query.

plugins.name: account_history
plugins.description: Tracks operations by account and provides per-account operation history queries.

methods.description: Returns a map of operations for a given account in the sequence range [from-limit, from]. Each account operation has a sequence number starting from 0. Use from=-1 (4294967295) to get the most recent operations.

params.name: account
params.caption: Account Name
params.description: The account name whose operation history to retrieve.

params.name: from
params.caption: From Sequence
params.description: The absolute sequence number. Use -1 (4294967295) for the most recent operation.

params.name: limit
params.caption: Limit
params.description: Maximum number of operations to return. Must be between 1 and 1000. Must be less than 'from' unless from is -1.

returns.description: Map of sequence number to applied_operation objects for the account.

plugins.name: operation_history
plugins.description: Tracks all blockchain operations and provides block-level and transaction-level operation queries.

methods.description: Returns the sequence of operations included or generated within a particular block. Virtual operations are generated by the blockchain (e.g. rewards) as opposed to user-submitted operations.

params.name: block_num
params.caption: Block Number
params.description: Height of the block whose operations should be returned.

params.name: only_virtual
params.caption: Only Virtual
params.description: Whether to only include virtual operations in the returned results.

returns.description: Array of applied_operation objects from the specified block.

methods.description: Returns a transaction by its ID, including block number and transaction index within the block.

params.name: id
params.caption: Transaction ID
params.description: The hash (SHA-256 / ripemd160) of the transaction to retrieve.

returns.description: annotated_signed_transaction with block_num and transaction_num fields added.

plugins.name: database_api
plugins.description: The core read-only API for the blockchain database. Provides access to blocks, accounts, chain properties, authority validation, vesting delegations, and more.

methods.description: Retrieves a block header by block number.

params.name: block_num
params.caption: Block Number
params.description: Height of the block whose header should be returned.

returns.description: The block header, or null if no matching block was found.

methods.description: Retrieves a full, signed block by block number.

params.name: block_num
params.caption: Block Number
params.description: Height of the block to be returned.

returns.description: The full signed block, or null if no matching block was found.

methods.description: Retrieves a block header only if the block is irreversible. Returns null if the block has not yet been finalized.

params.name: block_num
params.caption: Block Number
params.description: Height of the block whose header should be returned.

returns.description: The block header if the block is irreversible, or null.

methods.description: Retrieves a full, signed block only if it is irreversible. Returns null if the block has not yet been finalized.

params.name: block_num
params.caption: Block Number
params.description: Height of the block to be returned.

returns.description: The full signed block if irreversible, or null.

methods.description: Sets a callback function that is triggered on each newly generated block. Used for real-time block notifications via WebSocket.

params.name: callback
params.caption: Callback
params.description: Callback function to invoke when a new block is applied.

returns.description: No return value (callback-based).

methods.description: Retrieves compile-time constants and configuration values of the blockchain (e.g., chain ID, symbol, precision).

returns.description: Object containing blockchain compile-time configuration constants.

methods.description: Retrieves the current dynamic global properties object, which contains real-time chain state such as head block number, total supply, and other dynamic metrics.

returns.description: The dynamic_global_property_api_object with current chain state.

methods.description: Retrieves the chain properties as set by the median validator schedule (chain-wide constraints like account creation fee, maximum block size, etc.).

returns.description: chain_api_properties object with median chain parameters.

methods.description: Returns the current hardfork version of the blockchain.

returns.description: The current hardfork version string (e.g. '0.23.0').

methods.description: Returns the next scheduled hardfork version and the time it is planned to go live.

returns.description: Object with hf_version (string) and live_time (ISO timestamp).

methods.description: Returns full account objects for a list of account names. Includes balances, vesting, authority, and validator votes.

params.name: names
params.caption: Account Names
params.description: Array of account names to look up.

returns.description: Array of account_api_object entries. Only accounts that exist are returned.

methods.description: Looks up accounts by their names. Returns an optional account object for each name; null if the account does not exist.

params.name: account_names
params.caption: Account Names
params.description: Array of account names to look up.

returns.description: Array of optional account_api_object entries. Each element may be null.

methods.description: Looks up account names starting from a lower bound. Returns a set of account names in alphabetical order.

params.name: lower_bound_name
params.caption: Lower Bound Name
params.description: The lower bound of the first account name to return.

params.name: limit
params.caption: Limit
params.description: Maximum number of results to return. Must not exceed 1000.

returns.description: Set of account names matching the query.

methods.description: Returns the total number of accounts registered on the blockchain.

returns.description: Total number of registered accounts.

methods.description: Returns the master authority change history for a given account, useful for account recovery audits.

params.name: account
params.caption: Account Name
params.description: The account name whose master authority history to retrieve.

returns.description: Array of master_authority_history_api_object entries.

methods.description: Returns the current account recovery request for an account, if one exists.

params.name: account
params.caption: Account Name
params.description: The account name whose recovery request to check.

returns.description: The account_recovery_request_api_object, or null if no request exists.

methods.description: Returns the escrow object for a given sender and escrow ID.

params.name: from
params.caption: From Account
params.description: The account name of the escrow sender.

params.name: escrow_id
params.caption: Escrow ID
params.description: The numeric escrow ID to look up.

returns.description: The escrow_api_object, or null if not found.

methods.description: Returns vesting withdrawal routes for a given account. Can filter by direction (incoming, outgoing, or all).

params.name: account
params.caption: Account Name
params.description: The account name whose withdrawal routes to retrieve.

params.name: type
params.caption: Route Type
params.description: Filter direction: 'incoming', 'outgoing', or 'all'.

returns.description: Array of withdraw_route objects with from_account, to_account, percent, auto_vest.

methods.description: Returns vesting delegation objects for a given account. Supports pagination and filtering by delegated or received.

params.name: account
params.caption: Account Name
params.description: The delegator or delegatee account name.

params.name: from
params.caption: From
params.description: The account name to start from for pagination.

params.name: limit
params.caption: Limit
params.description: Maximum number of results. Defaults to 100. Must not exceed 1000.

params.name: type
params.caption: Delegation Type
params.description: Filter type: 'delegated' (sent) or 'received'. Defaults to 'delegated'.

returns.description: Array of vesting_delegation_api_object entries.

methods.description: Returns expiring vesting delegation objects for a given account, starting from a given date.

params.name: account
params.caption: Account Name
params.description: The delegator account name.

params.name: from
params.caption: From Date
params.description: Start date/time for expiration lookup (ISO timestamp).

params.name: limit
params.caption: Limit
params.description: Maximum number of results. Defaults to 100. Must not exceed 1000.

returns.description: Array of vesting_delegation_expiration_api_object entries.

methods.description: Returns a hexadecimal dump of the serialized binary form of a transaction.

params.name: trx
params.caption: Transaction
params.description: The signed transaction object to serialize.

returns.description: Hex-encoded serialized transaction.

methods.description: Given a partially signed transaction and a set of available public keys, returns the minimal subset of public keys that should add signatures to authorize the transaction.

params.name: trx
params.caption: Transaction
params.description: The signed transaction to analyze.

params.name: available_keys
params.caption: Available Keys
params.description: Array/set of public keys that the caller can sign with.

returns.description: Set of public keys that are required to sign the transaction.

methods.description: Returns the set of all public keys that could possibly sign for a given transaction. Useful for wallets to filter their key set before calling get_required_signatures.

params.name: trx
params.caption: Transaction
params.description: The signed transaction to analyze.

returns.description: Set of all public keys that could potentially authorize the transaction.

methods.description: Verifies that a transaction has all of the required signatures. Returns true if valid, otherwise throws an exception.

params.name: trx
params.caption: Transaction
params.description: The signed transaction to verify.

returns.description: true if the transaction has all required signatures.

methods.description: Verifies that a set of public keys has sufficient authority to authorize actions on behalf of an account.

params.name: name_or_id
params.caption: Account Name
params.description: The account name to check authority for.

params.name: signers
params.caption: Signer Keys
params.description: Array/set of public keys to verify against the account's authority.

returns.description: true if the signers have enough authority to authorize the account.

methods.description: Returns database shared memory usage information including total size, free size, reserved size, used size, and per-index record counts.

returns.description: Object with total_size, free_size, reserved_size, used_size, and index_list (array of {name, record_count}).

methods.description: Returns proposed transactions (proposals) associated with a given account, both authored and requiring approval.

params.name: account
params.caption: Account Name
params.description: The account name whose proposals to retrieve.

params.name: from
params.caption: From Offset
params.description: Offset for pagination (number of results to skip).

params.name: limit
params.caption: Limit
params.description: Maximum number of proposals to return. Must not exceed 100.

returns.description: Array of proposal_api_object entries.

methods.description: Returns a list of accounts currently on sale (direct sale, not auction). Only accounts whose sale start time has passed are included.

params.name: from
params.caption: From Offset
params.description: Number of results to skip for pagination.

params.name: limit
params.caption: Limit
params.description: Maximum number of results to return. Must not exceed 1000.

returns.description: Array of account_on_sale_api_object entries.

methods.description: Returns a list of accounts currently on auction (no target buyer set). Only accounts whose sale start time has passed are included.

params.name: from
params.caption: From Offset
params.description: Number of results to skip for pagination.

params.name: limit
params.caption: Limit
params.description: Maximum number of results to return. Must not exceed 1000.

returns.description: Array of account_on_sale_api_object entries for auction listings.

methods.description: Returns a list of subaccounts currently on sale.

params.name: from
params.caption: From Offset
params.description: Number of results to skip for pagination.

params.name: limit
params.caption: Limit
params.description: Maximum number of results to return. Must not exceed 1000.

returns.description: Array of subaccount_on_sale_api_object entries.

plugins.name: account_by_key
plugins.description: Provides a lookup from public keys to the accounts that reference those keys in their authority.

methods.description: Returns all account names that reference the given public keys in their master, active, or regular authority.

params.name: keys
params.caption: Public Keys
params.description: Array of public keys to look up.

returns.description: Array of arrays of account names. Each inner array corresponds to one input key and contains all accounts referencing that key.

plugins.name: network_broadcast_api
plugins.description: Provides transaction and block broadcasting capabilities. This is the write API for submitting transactions to the network.

methods.description: Broadcasts a signed transaction to the network. The transaction is accepted into the pending pool and propagated to P2P peers. Optionally checks that the blockchain is not too far behind.

params.name: trx
params.caption: Transaction
params.description: The signed transaction to broadcast.

params.name: max_block_age
params.caption: Max Block Age
params.description: Optional. Maximum allowed age of the head block in seconds. If the blockchain is behind by more than this, the call will fail. Use -1 to disable.

returns.description: No return value on success.

methods.description: Broadcasts a signed transaction and waits for confirmation. Returns the transaction ID, block number, and transaction index once included in a block. The callback includes whether the transaction expired.

params.name: trx
params.caption: Transaction
params.description: The signed transaction to broadcast.

params.name: max_block_age
params.caption: Max Block Age
params.description: Optional. Maximum allowed age of the head block in seconds. Use -1 to disable.

returns.description: Object with id (transaction hash), block_num, trx_num, and expired fields.

methods.description: Broadcasts a signed block to the network. Typically used by validators to propagate newly produced blocks.

params.name: block
params.caption: Block
params.description: The signed block to broadcast.

returns.description: No return value on success.

methods.description: Broadcasts a signed transaction with a confirmation callback. The first argument is the callback, followed by the transaction. Similar to broadcast_transaction_synchronous but with custom callback handling.

params.name: callback
params.caption: Callback
params.description: Confirmation callback function.

params.name: trx
params.caption: Transaction
params.description: The signed transaction to broadcast.

params.name: max_block_age
params.caption: Max Block Age
params.description: Optional. Maximum allowed age of the head block in seconds. Use -1 to disable.

returns.description: No direct return; result delivered via callback.

plugins.name: committee_api
plugins.description: Provides access to committee worker proposal requests and their voting state.

methods.description: Returns a committee request by its ID, optionally including votes.

params.name: request_id
params.caption: Request ID
params.description: The numeric ID of the committee request to retrieve.

params.name: votes_count
params.caption: Votes Count
params.description: Number of votes to include. Use 0 for no votes, -1 for all votes, or a positive number to limit.

returns.description: committee_api_object with optional embedded votes array.

methods.description: Returns all votes for a specific committee request.

params.name: request_id
params.caption: Request ID
params.description: The numeric ID of the committee request whose votes to retrieve.

returns.description: Array of committee_vote_state objects.

methods.description: Returns a list of committee request IDs filtered by status.

params.name: status
params.caption: Status
params.description: The status code to filter by (e.g. 0=pending, 1=approved, etc.).

returns.description: Array of committee request IDs matching the given status.

plugins.name: invite_api
plugins.description: Provides access to invite objects used for account registration via invite keys.

methods.description: Returns a list of invite IDs filtered by status.

params.name: status
params.caption: Status
params.description: The status code to filter invites by.

returns.description: Array of invite database IDs matching the given status.

methods.description: Returns an invite object by its database ID.

params.name: id
params.caption: Invite ID
params.description: The database ID of the invite to retrieve.

returns.description: invite_api_object with invite details (key, creator, balance, etc.).

methods.description: Returns an invite object by its public key.

params.name: key
params.caption: Invite Key
params.description: The public key associated with the invite.

returns.description: invite_api_object matching the given key.

plugins.name: paid_subscription_api
plugins.description: Provides access to paid subscription data: subscription options set by content creators, subscription status of subscribers, and active/inactive subscription lists.

methods.description: Returns the paid subscription settings for a given account (creator).

params.name: account
params.caption: Account Name
params.description: The account name of the subscription creator.

returns.description: paid_subscription_state with subscription details (price, period, etc.).

methods.description: Returns a paginated list of all paid subscription objects.

params.name: from
params.caption: From Offset
params.description: Number of results to skip for pagination.

params.name: limit
params.caption: Limit
params.description: Maximum number of results to return. Must not exceed 1000.

returns.description: Array of paid_subscription_object entries.

methods.description: Returns the subscription status of a specific subscriber for a given creator account.

params.name: subscriber
params.caption: Subscriber
params.description: The account name of the subscriber.

params.name: account
params.caption: Creator Account
params.description: The account name of the subscription creator.

returns.description: paid_subscribe_state with subscription status details.

methods.description: Returns a list of creator account names that a given subscriber has active subscriptions to.

params.name: subscriber
params.caption: Subscriber
params.description: The account name of the subscriber.

returns.description: Array of creator account names with active subscriptions.

methods.description: Returns a list of creator account names that a given subscriber has inactive (expired) subscriptions to.

params.name: subscriber
params.caption: Subscriber
params.description: The account name of the subscriber.

returns.description: Array of creator account names with inactive subscriptions.

plugins.name: custom_protocol_api
plugins.description: Provides access to account data enriched with custom protocol sequence information. Custom protocols allow third-party applications to track per-account custom operations.

methods.description: Returns an account object enriched with custom protocol sequence data for a specific custom protocol ID. Populates custom_sequence and custom_sequence_block_num fields.

params.name: account
params.caption: Account Name
params.description: The account name to look up.

params.name: custom_protocol_id
params.caption: Custom Protocol ID
params.description: The custom protocol ID string to retrieve the sequence for. Use empty string to skip custom protocol lookup.

returns.description: account_api_object with custom_sequence and custom_sequence_block_num populated for the given protocol.

plugins.name: auth_util
plugins.description: Provides utility methods for verifying account authority signatures against arbitrary data digests.

methods.description: Verifies that the provided signatures are valid for the given account's authority at a specified level (master, active, or regular). Returns the public keys derived from the signatures.

params.name: account_name
params.caption: Account Name
params.description: The account name whose authority to check.

params.name: level
params.caption: Authority Level
params.description: The authority level to verify against: 'master' (or 'm'), 'active' (or 'a'), 'regular' (or 'r'). Empty string defaults to 'active'.

params.name: dig
params.caption: Digest
params.description: The SHA-256 hash of the data that was signed.

params.name: sigs
params.caption: Signatures
params.description: Array of signatures to verify.

returns.description: Array of public keys recovered from the valid signatures.

plugins.name: block_info
plugins.description: Tracks block metadata (size, average block size, slot info) and provides queries to retrieve this information for ranges of blocks.

methods.description: Returns block metadata (block_id, block_size, average_block_size, aslot, last_irreversible_block_num) for a range of blocks starting from start_block_num.

params.name: start_block_num
params.caption: Start Block Number
params.description: The first block number to return info for. Must be greater than 0.

params.name: count
params.caption: Count
params.description: Number of blocks to return info for. Must not exceed 10000.

returns.description: Array of block_info objects. Entries may be empty if no info is stored (e.g. blocks before snapshot).

methods.description: Returns full signed blocks with attached metadata for a range. Limits total response size to 8 MB. Stops early if no info is stored for a block.

params.name: start_block_num
params.caption: Start Block Number
params.description: The first block number to return. Must be greater than 0.

params.name: count
params.caption: Count
params.description: Maximum number of blocks to return. Must not exceed 10000. Response is capped at 8 MB total.

returns.description: Array of block_with_info objects, each containing a signed block and its block_info metadata.

plugins.name: raw_block
plugins.description: Provides access to raw (base64-encoded) serialized block data for low-level block inspection or re-import.

methods.description: Returns a raw block by block number, including the base64-encoded serialized binary, block ID, previous block ID, and timestamp.

params.name: block_num
params.caption: Block Number
params.description: Height of the block to retrieve in raw form.

returns.description: Object with block_id, previous, timestamp, and raw_block (base64-encoded string) fields.
